name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow, Inc. provides a cloud-based platform for digital workflows across IT, HR, customer service, security, and enterprise operations. The Now Platform enables organizations to automate workflows, manage IT services, and deploy AI agents at enterprise scale. Founded in 2004 and headquartered in Santa Clara, California, ServiceNow serves government, financial services, healthcare, and technology sectors with approximately $14B in annual revenue.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has transformed from an ITSM ticketing tool into the defining enterprise AI workflow platform, achieving 22% subscription revenue growth at $15B+ scale [1]. The $7.75B Armis acquisition (April 2026) tripled the security and risk TAM, and Now Assist AI products surpassed $200M ACV with the largest deal exceeding $20M [1][2]. This represents a complete business model evolution. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist is shipping with nearly 1,000 AI customers, $200M+ ACV as of December 2025, and a $1B AI ACV target for 2026 [2]. Pro Plus deal volumes quadrupled in ITSM and CSM, HRSD doubled, and ITOM Plus tripled — concrete evidence of AI-driven revenue acceleration. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow is the undisputed leader in IT Service Management (Gartner Magic Quadrant leader consistently), with 603 customers above $5M ACV (up 20% YoY) and 244 transactions over $1M in Q4 2025 [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): The agentic AI roadmap is described as 'the most ambitious in enterprise software,' with Armis integration expanding into security, but specific next-gen product launch dates beyond general 2026 roadmap are limited [2]. R&D Intensity (1/1): ServiceNow invests heavily in R&D, having spent 6+ years building AI capabilities on the Now Platform, with ongoing investment in agentic AI, workflow automation, and the Armis integration [2].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose — 'The world works with ServiceNow' — under Bill McDermott has been articulated as making work better for everyone through the Now Platform [2]. McDermott's philosophy of 'people before profits' and the vision to be 'the Defining AI Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century' is specific, thoughtful, and drives strategic decisions (Armis acquisition, AI investment, platform expansion). However, McDermott joined in 2019, so the purpose hasn't been tested through multi-decade consistency or leadership transitions. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow donated $1M to decarbonization nonprofits, has global impact ambassadors in all regions, and was named to Ethisphere's 2026 World's Most Ethical Companies list [3]. General corporate philanthropy rather than distinctive programs. Employee Retention (2/2): Glassdoor rating of 4.1/5 with 81% of employees recommending ServiceNow to a friend, Culture & Values at 4.2/5, and Work-Life Balance at 4.0/5 — consistently above average for enterprise software [2]. Insider Buying (0/2): No specific net insider buying data identified. McDermott's employment agreement was extended through 2030, signaling commitment, but insider buying evidence was not found.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 delivered $3,770M revenue (up 22% YoY) with 32% non-GAAP operating margin and 44% free cash flow margin [1]. D/E of 20.7% is well below the 70% target. ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target. Revenue growth of 22.1% YoY and 22.4% 3-year CAGR both exceed the 10% target at maximum score. Forward P/E of 20.2 is essentially at the 20 target (0.995 score). Profit margin of 12.6% is below the 20% target but reflects heavy investment in AI and Armis integration. Forward PEG of 0.98 is below the 1.0 ideal. Total RPO of $27.7B (up 25% YoY) and cRPO of $12.64B (up 22.5%) provide exceptional forward revenue visibility [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): ServiceNow IS the enterprise AI infrastructure — its Now Platform is the orchestration layer that connects AI agents to enterprise workflows across IT, HR, customer service, and security [2]. The company cannot be disrupted by AI because it is building the platform through which enterprises deploy AI. As McDermott states, 'While AI thinks, workflow acts,' highlighting that AI needs workflow context and security to perform real-world tasks, and ServiceNow provides exactly that [2]. Analysts project agentic AI will capture 30% of enterprise software revenue by 2035, with ServiceNow as an early mover. The 81.5% subscription gross margin and deep customer integration (603 customers above $5M ACV) create structural switching costs. AI Opportunity (1/1): ServiceNow generates quantifiable AI revenue — $200M+ ACV from Now Assist Pro Plus products with a $1B AI ACV target for 2026, making AI a direct and rapidly growing revenue contributor [1][2].

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under Bill McDermott demonstrates strong infinite game characteristics — a bold vision to define AI enterprise software, willingness to make large bets ($7.75B Armis acquisition), and genuine investment in employee culture (81% Glassdoor recommend rate). The 'people before profits' philosophy drives strategic decisions. However, McDermott has only led since 2019 and the company's Just Cause hasn't been tested through a leadership transition or crisis. The Armis acquisition, while bold, also reflects a financial engineering approach (acquiring revenue) alongside organic innovation.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): McDermott's vision to make ServiceNow 'the Defining AI Enterprise Software Company of the 21st Century' is specific, ambitious, and shapes strategic decisions — the Armis acquisition, AI investment, and platform expansion all trace back to this vision [2]. The 'people before profits' philosophy adds depth. However, this Just Cause is relatively new (McDermott joined 2019) and hasn't survived a leadership transition or major crisis to earn 3/3.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Glassdoor's 4.1/5 overall rating with 81% recommend and 4.2 Culture & Values score indicates a healthy organizational culture above industry norms [2]. Employees describe a supportive, collaborative environment with strong learning opportunities. Some concerns about management practices and long hours prevent a perfect score. McDermott's employment extension through 2030 provides leadership stability.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): ServiceNow's competitive posture focuses on building the best platform rather than defeating specific rivals. The 'While AI thinks, workflow acts' positioning acknowledges AI platform competitors' strengths while differentiating on workflow context and enterprise integration [2]. The Armis acquisition expanded into security to improve capabilities rather than simply block competitors.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): ServiceNow evolved significantly from an ITSM ticketing tool to a comprehensive enterprise workflow platform, then pivoted to become an AI orchestration platform — each step building on the prior foundation [2]. The $7.75B Armis acquisition entering cybersecurity represents meaningful expansion. These are substantial evolutionary steps, but the core platform model has been consistent rather than a transformative pivot that abandoned a profitable business.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): McDermott has demonstrated a pattern of courageous bets — investing heavily in AI for 6+ years before the market validated it, committing to agentic AI as the company's future, and making the $7.75B Armis acquisition (the largest in company history) to expand the security TAM [1][2]. The company raised full-year guidance after Q1 despite absorbing 75bps operating margin headwind from Armis, demonstrating confidence in long-term capability over short-term margin optimization. McDermott's 'most ambitious agentic AI roadmap in enterprise software' represents a genuine bet on the future.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow delivered exceptional Q1 2026 results — $3,770M revenue (up 22% YoY), 32% non-GAAP operating margin, and 44% free cash flow margin — beating guidance across all topline and profitability metrics and raising full-year subscription revenue guidance to $15.7-15.8B [1]. With $27.7B in total RPO (up 25% YoY) providing multi-year revenue visibility, $200M+ AI ACV targeting $1B by 2026, and the $7.75B Armis acquisition tripling the security TAM, ServiceNow is executing the most compelling AI monetization story in enterprise software [1][2]. At a Forward PEG of 0.98, the stock is fairly valued relative to its 22%+ growth rate, with 81.5% subscription gross margins and $5.1B free cash flow providing exceptional financial quality.
thesis_continued: The primary risk is valuation compression in a risk-off environment — at a trailing P/E of 60.3, any deceleration in subscription growth from the current 22% trajectory could trigger significant multiple compression, particularly as the Armis acquisition creates near-term headwinds to operating margin (75bps) and FCF margin (200bps) [1]. GAAP profit margin of 12.6% remains modest relative to the premium valuation, and stock-based compensation inflates non-GAAP metrics. ServiceNow's dependence on enterprise IT spending makes it cyclically exposed to budget freezes during economic slowdowns, and competition from Microsoft (Copilot for Service), Salesforce (Einstein), and emerging AI-native workflow platforms creates competitive risk [2]. The $7.75B Armis integration carries execution risk as ServiceNow enters the unfamiliar security market.
key_catalysts: Achievement of $1B AI ACV target in 2026, validating the agentic AI monetization thesis and demonstrating that Now Assist is driving incremental rather than cannibalistic revenue growth
Successful Armis integration tripling the security and risk solutions TAM and opening cross-sell opportunities across ServiceNow's 603 customers with $5M+ ACV
Acceleration of agentic AI adoption across enterprise workflows as organizations move from AI pilots to production deployments, with ServiceNow positioned as the orchestration layer
key_risks: Trailing P/E of 60.3 creates significant downside if subscription growth decelerates below 20%, as the premium valuation leaves minimal margin for error on quarterly results

Armis acquisition integration risk — the $7.75B deal creates near-term margin headwinds (75bps operating, 200bps FCF) and enters ServiceNow into the competitive cybersecurity market for the first time
Competition from Microsoft Copilot for Service, Salesforce Einstein, and AI-native workflow startups threatening ServiceNow's positioning as the enterprise AI orchestration platform
sources: ServiceNow Q1 2026 Financial Results, Investor Presentation, and Full-Year Guidance — servicenow.com Investor Relations, April 2026
Bill McDermott Leadership, AI Strategy (Now Assist, Agentic AI), and Armis Acquisition — BBN Times, BYDFi, ainvest.com, io-fund.com, 2025-2026
ServiceNow ESG — Ethisphere 2026 Most Ethical Companies, Net-Zero by 2030, Glassdoor Reviews — servicenow.com, Glassdoor, 2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed June 2026
